action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 20 achieved 79.76%, far below the best of 83.11%. The persistent head approach clearly underperforms compared to the successful runs from iterations 8-10. Analysis: The training curve from iter_8 shows train_acc starting high and dropping moderately at task boundaries, which is inconsistent with random head reinitialization (that would drop to ~10%). This suggests iter 8 likely used persistent head OR the head reinitialization in later iterations was broken. More importantly, LBFGS with explicit fresh-head creation got 81.89% (iter 18), proving fresh heads help. The AdamW attempts that got stuck at ~78% probably failed because they called reset_parameters() on a custom CosineLinear module that lacks it, so the head was never actually reset. Decision: Rewrite model.py/train.py/predict.py from scratch with a guaranteed-fresh head each task (instantiate new CosineLinear), full feature replay, AdamW+cosine, label smoothing 0.1, feature noise 0.01, 20 epochs. Save full model state dict in checkpoint for robust offline loading. Keep horizontal-flip TTA in predict.py. Run training, verify predict.py locally on val.pt, then submit eval.
action.command: python train.py && mkdir -p dummy_test && ln -sf $RAB_DATA_DIR/val.pt dummy_test/test.pt && python predict.py --data_path dummy_test --checkpoint checkpoints/model.pt --output test_out.json && python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('test_out.json')); assert len(d['predictions'])==5000; print('Verification passed, preds:', len(d['predictions']))"
